title: toolbox: failed to start container fedora-toolbox-RELEASEVER
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

toolbox: failed to start container fedora-toolbox-RELEASEVER

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `toolbox create` command and create a box.

2. And run the box `toolbox enter`


**Describe the results you received:**
toolbox says "failed to start container fedora-toolbox-37"

**Describe the results you expected:**
toolbox running smoothly

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0-rc3
API Version:  4.2.0-rc3
Go Version:   go1.19
Built:        Fri Aug  5 22:53:26 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Addition: 

Output of `podman --log-level debug start fedora-toolbox-37`
```
...
DEBU[0000] Created OCI spec for container 2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec at /home/fedora/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec/userdata/config.json 
DEBU[0000] /usr/bin/conmon messages will be logged to syslog 
DEBU[0000] running conmon: /usr/bin/conmon               args="[--api-version 1 -c 2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec -u 2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec -r /usr/bin/crun -b /home/fedora/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec/userdata -p /run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec/userdata/pidfile -n fedora-toolbox-37 --exit-dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/exits --full-attach -s -l journald --log-level debug --syslog --conmon-pidfile /run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec/userdata/conmon.pid --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /home/fedora/.local/share/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/containers --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg debug --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg systemd --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp --exit-command-arg --network-config-dir --exit-command-arg  --exit-command-arg --network-backend --exit-command-arg netavark --exit-command-arg --volumepath --exit-command-arg /home/fedora/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg crun --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg overlay --exit-command-arg --events-backend --exit-command-arg journald --exit-command-arg --syslog --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg 2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec]"
[conmon:d]: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied

DEBU[0000] Received: -1                                 
DEBU[0000] Cleaning up container 2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec 
DEBU[0000] Network is already cleaned up, skipping...   
DEBU[0000] Unmounted container "2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec" 
Error: OCI runtime error: unable to start container "2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec": crun: sd-bus call: Unit libpod-2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec.scope already exists.: File exists
```

it looks like there is already another unit with that name.

What is the output of `systemctl --user status libpod-2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec.scope`?

> systemctl --user status libpod-2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec.scope

```
× libpod-2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec.scope - libcrun container
     Loaded: loaded (/run/user/1000/systemd/transient/libpod-2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec.scope; transient)
  Transient: yes
     Active: failed (Result: resources)
        CPU: 0

Aug 10 10:19:38 190502 systemd[1639]: libpod-2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec.scope: Couldn't move process 28239 to requested cgroup '/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/user.slice/libpod-2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec.scope' (directly or via the system bus): Permission denied
Aug 10 10:19:38 190502 systemd[1639]: libpod-2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec.scope: Failed to add PIDs to scope's control group: Permission denied
Aug 10 10:19:38 190502 systemd[1639]: libpod-2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec.scope: Failed with result 'resources'.
Aug 10 10:19:38 190502 systemd[1639]: Failed to start libpod-2ce22b06eace2b0eb0f795b95f8f8a84c7e315c09ac4be2a5a703f0b468cd3ec.scope - libcrun container.
```

thanks, so systemd cannot move the new process to the target cgroup.

Can you show the output of `cat /proc/self/cgroup` and give it another try disabling temporarily SELinux with `setenforce 0` (after you test you can enable it again with `setenforce 1`)?

> thanks, so systemd cannot move the new process to the target cgroup.
> 
> Can you show the output of `cat /proc/self/cgroup` and give it another try disabling temporarily SELinux with `setenforce 0` (after you test you can enable it again with `setenforce 1`)?

```
❯ cat /proc/self/cgroup 
0::/user.slice/user-1000.slice/session-1.scope

❯ sudo setenforce 0
[sudo] password for fedora: 

❯ toolbox enter
Error: failed to start container fedora-toolbox-37

❯ sudo setenforce 1

❯ toolbox enter
Error: failed to start container fedora-toolbox-37
```

do you get the same error if you do `systemd-run --scope --user echo hello`?

Can you spot anything useful in the journal (`journalctl --user -e`)?

> do you get the same error if you do `systemd-run --scope --user echo hello`?
> 
> Can you spot anything useful in the journal (`journalctl --user -e`)?

```
Running scope as unit: run-rda084a86f67344608f20e44644c08160.scope
hello
```

```
Aug 10 11:56:11 190502 systemd[1639]: Started run-rda084a86f67344608f20e44644c08160.scope - /usr/bin/echo hello.
```

could you try `systemd-run --scope --user podman --log-level debug start fedora-toolbox-37`?

How have you configured your system?  It is a fresh installation of Rawhide?  Are you ssh'ing into it?

> systemd-run --scope --user podman --log-level debug start fedora-toolbox-37

Not work

> How have you configured your system? It is a fresh installation of Rawhide? Are you ssh'ing into it?

I upgraded system from f36

is the container freshly created or is it old?  Could you try recreating it?

Do other podman containers work fine?

I am not able to reproduce this issue on Rawhide.

Are you ssh'ing into the Rawhide machine or what environment are you using?

> is the container freshly created or is it old? Could you try recreating it?
 
Yes, I deleted the container and recreate it

> Do other podman containers work fine?

I tried to create a debian container with distrobox. The same problem existed there

> I am not able to reproduce this issue on Rawhide.

> Are you ssh'ing into the Rawhide machine or what environment are you using?

I'm using Fedora 37 on my own computer
```
OS : Fedora Linux 37 (Rawhide Prerelease) x86_64 
KR : 5.19.0-65.fc37.x86_64 
PK : 2015 (rpm), 54 (flatpak) 
SH : fish 3.5.0 
WM : sway 
TE : kitty 
MEM: 3210MiB / 7841MiB
```
I've tried to recreate your environment using sway+fish+kitty on Rawhide with the same kernel but still I cannot reproduce the issue
Interesting :thinking:

> I've tried to recreate your environment using sway+fish+kitty on Rawhide with the same kernel but still I cannot reproduce the issue

I installed Fedora 37 from scratch. But I got the same error. I suspect there is a missing package(s)
are the `dbus-broker.service` and `user@1000.service` service running correctly?

> are the `dbus-broker.service` and `user@1000.service` service running correctly?

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/62564400/184115700-ea842f2f-3058-41d9-a541-e5e4c952e672.png)

I've installed Rawhide again from scratch and I am still not able to see the issue you've reported, so it must be some different configuration or missing package.

Can you reproduce on a standard Fedora installation?

> Can you reproduce on a standard Fedora installation?

I didn't understand
sway+fish+kitty are not part of the default installation
I did a normal installation inside the virtual machine and did not encounter any problems.

@giuseppe 

We messed with @onuralpszr and found that the problem was with the toolbox. Toolbox goes to wrong dbus address and throws an error. When you define the address manually with the `DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=unix:path=/run/user/$(id -u)/bus` variable, the problem disappears. We will need to report this issue under the containers/toolbox project

Well, I didn't call "messed" preferably saying "worked" would be better choice plus you have to say that your system is "fedora everything installation" so some of your config yours was not there I don't know that variable should be exist under normal installation of podman or toolbox but closing without proper discussion not a good idea as well. 

@giuseppe  ptal please.
